title: Tapping for the Anniversary Wave
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the grief that returns on dates and in seasons
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for grief that returns on a date: the birthday, the anniversary of the death, the season they loved. For the feeling of being ambushed by loss when you thought you were further along.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when a date or season brings grief back with unexpected force, particularly when you thought you had moved past the sharpest part.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for anniversary grief: the wave that arrives on a specific date or in a particular season and catches you off guard. The script works with the shock of being ambushed, the frustration of still feeling this, and the presence of the loss underneath the calendar.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though the grief has come back today on this date and I thought I was further along than this, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am frustrated and sad and the wave feels as strong as ever, I accept what I am feeling right now.
Even though the dates know something my ordinary days forget, I am allowed to feel all of this.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The date that carries their absence.
Side of Eye: The shock of being caught off guard again.
Under Eye: Expecting to be done with this by now.
Under Nose: The body that remembers even when the mind has moved on.
Chin: The specific shape of missing them on this day.
Collarbone: The grief that the calendar knows.
Under Arm: The frustration at still being here with this feeling.
Top of Head: Allowing the wave to be what it is.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: The wave means they still matter. That is not a problem.
Side of Eye: Grief that returns on dates is grief doing what grief does.
Under Eye: I don't have to be further along. I am where I am.
Under Nose: I can sit with this feeling without it meaning something is wrong with me.
Chin: The love underneath this grief is still present.
Collarbone: This day is theirs. I can hold that gently.
Under Arm: The wave will pass. It always does. I know this.
Top of Head: I am carrying this with care. That is enough for today.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
